title: Rudgea minor subsp. calycina
what_description: Rudgea minor subsp. calycina is a plant that belongs to the family Rubiaceae. It is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height, with a woody stem and branches. The leaves are elliptical in shape, 2-5 cm long, and 0.5-1.2 cm wide, with a cuneate base and a rounded apex. The flowers are axillary, in a corymbiform cyme, with a calyx of 3-4 mm in length and a corolla of 5-6 mm in length. The flowers are white with a yellow center, and they bloom from January to June.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Rudgea minor subsp. calycina are inflammations, which the plant effectively reduces through its anti-inflammatory properties, fever, caused by its antipyretic properties, diarrhea, which it treats by stabilizing the gut flora, skin irritations, including eczema and dermatitis, that the plant soothes by reducing inflammation and promoting skin regeneration, as well as wound healing, facilitated by its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties. Rudgea minor subsp. calycina also exhibits antibacterial properties, which combat bacterial infections such as urinary tract infections. The plant's pain relief properties, stemming from its analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds, alleviate rheumatism and arthritis. Moreover, its antimicrobial properties protect against fungal infections like candidiasis. Additionally, its antiviral properties and antimicrobial activity combat viral infections, gastroenteritis, cough, and infections like pneumonia, while its antioxidant properties protect against oxidative damage.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Rudgea minor subsp. calycina are its antioxidant properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids such as quercetin and kaempferol, allowing it to neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. Its antimicrobial properties are also noteworthy, thanks to the bioactive compounds such as saponins and alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various microorganisms, including bacteria and fungi. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties are mediated by the action of glycosides and phenolic acids, which can reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. Rudgea minor subsp. calycina's analgesic properties are thought to be due to the presence of volatile oils and terpenes, which can help to relieve pain and reduce discomfort. Additionally, its antifungal properties, which are attributed to the action of sesquiterpenes and diterpenes, make it a potential candidate for the treatment of fungal infections.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Rudgea minor subsp. calycina are alkaloids, flavonoids, phenolic acids, glycosides, and saponins which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Alkaloids, such as ajmalicine and ajmalin, possess anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic activities while flavonoids, including quercetin and kaempferol, exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Phenolic acids, like caffeic acid and ferulic acid, demonstrate antibacterial and antifungal activities, while glycosides, including flavonoid glycosides, contribute to the plant's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Saponins, like oleanolic acid and ursolic acid, possess anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective activities, protecting the liver from damage. These constituents work synergistically to provide the medicinal benefits associated with Rudgea minor subsp. calycina, making it a valuable plant for traditional medicine.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Rudgea minor subsp. calycina are infusion, tincture, tea, decoction, and salve. Infusion is prepared by steeping the leaves in hot water to obtain its volatile oil, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are used for their antimicrobial properties. Tincture is made by soaking the leaves in a solvent, typically ethanol or glycerol, to extract its bioactive compounds, including phenolic acids and flavonoids, which are used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. Decoction involves boiling the plant material in water to release its alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are used for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Rudgea minor subsp. calycina are also used to prepare a salve, which is applied topically to soothe skin irritations and wounds, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Rudgea minor subsp. calycina improperly are experiencing nausea, inducing nausea, developing an allergic reaction, inducing vomiting, and causing diarrhea. These side effects can occur due to the plant's alkaloids, which can cause gastrointestinal irritation, allergic reactions, and other adverse effects. Rudgea minor subsp. calycina contains compounds such as indole alkaloids, which have been shown to cause nausea and vomiting in some individuals. Improper use of the plant can lead to an overdose of these alkaloids, resulting in severe gastrointestinal symptoms. It is essential to note that Rudgea minor subsp. calycina should only be used under the guidance of a medical professional due to its potential toxicity and side effects.
